text: iMessage integration flagged as most urgent. Week passes. Status: not started. If it's truly urgent, the issue is priority enforcement at the intake layer.
The /now API runs but returns wrong timestamp data. Built the automation before building the validation layer. Execution without accuracy is just noise.
Follow-up tracking exists. Sister Mallak doesn't respond. The system tracks but doesn't surface at decision points. Need escalation triggers.
RSVP system built. Result: 2 confirmations from many invites. The system captured failure but didn't prevent it. Missing pre-send probability scoring.
Three-tier awareness-planning-execution architecture documented. Execution tier consistently empty. The tiers don't feed each other. Architecture exists, data flow doesn't.
Ideas.md grows. No review time allocated. Classic accumulation without processing pipeline. Need scheduled review triggers, not wishful availability.
133 todo items. Morning briefing runs daily. But which integration surfaces the right 3 items at the right time? Reporting exists, prioritization doesn't.
Every three hours notification system designed. Implementation status: pending. Meanwhile manual context switching continues. The cost is cognitive, the fix is technical.
Cron automation for morning briefing would eliminate manual trigger. Not implemented. Still running it manually. The infrastructure exists, the final connection doesn't.
MAPS Ready volunteer database needed. Google forms to be prepared. Sign-up announcement pending. The recruitment funnel is designed but not deployed.
Kitchen reorganization for kids' plate access. Low-cost efficiency gain. Status: identified, not executed. Even manual improvements wait in queue behind system building.
Open Mosque debrief agenda complete before event execution. Planning architecture is solid. But why does planning get prioritized over the execution it's meant to support.
